fields.summary: Make RM provide heartbeat interval to NM
RM scheduler event handler thread gets behind

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body: This patch simply sends a heartbeat interval T to the NM from the RM in the heartbeat response. The value of T will be read from config. NM uses the value of T to schedule the next heartbeat. The very first NM heartbeat is sent as soon as possible after NM startup (which is what the code should already be doing currently).The accept/reject or dynamic heartbeat interval will be in next subtask.

Thanks for the early patch. Overall approach looks ok. Some comments.

getNextHeartbeatInterval() or simply getHeartbeatInterval() may be better names.
nextHeartbeatInterval or simply heartbeatInterval may be better names. 

please try to adhere to existing code style (similar to http://portals.apache.org/development/code-standards.html) Dont use tabs. Use 2 spaces for tabs and for indentation.

why is new exception being thrown in nodestatusUpdaterImpl?
{code}
+  private void registerWithRM() throws YarnRemoteException,IOException {
{code}

you would want to remove the existing NM-RM heartbeat config or mark is deprecated. heartbeatInterval can become a local variable initialized to some small hard-coded value.

Doesnt look like there is a need for RMTraffic. ResourceTrackerService can read default heartbeat interval from config during init() and then use that value.

The PBImpl changes seem incomplete since you still need to add the new field to the actual proto definition in yarn_server_common_protos.proto. And please look at existing long member values for guidance on other changes.

The expectation is to make the heartbeat interval dynamic, we should remove the old config and not even add in the new config? May be hard code it to 1 sec for now and immediately follow up in YARN-310 ..

I see the config being really a minimum value and not the default. ie. heartbeat interval can be increased under load but RM will try to come back to the minimum heartbeat interval for responsiveness. With that understanding, it may make sense to have it in a config when cluster-size/workload/other-factors make it impossible for the code to achieve a consistent 1 second response time under normal working conditions.
Add the test case to test configuration read correctly.

1. create new contents in YarnConfiguration, to set default value
2. Everytime ResourceTrackerService will set HeartBeatInterval, and NM will get and use this interval
3. add heartbeatInterval variable in .proto file

And it is not introduced by this patch.

Comments on the patch:
 - NM_TO_RM_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS should be removed completely, we aren't looking for back compat yet.
 - Rename heartbeat.interval-ms to nodemanagers.heartbeat-interval-ms and move the constants to be together with other RM constants.
 - yarn-default.xml: Fix the new property's description to be so: The heart-beat interval in milliseconds for every NodeManager in the cluster.
 - Rename NodeHeartbeatResponse.getHeartBeatInterval() to getNextHeartBeatInterval(). Similarly the setter.
 - NodeStatusUpdaterImpl: The initial heartbeat interval should be zero. Also rename heartBeatInterval to nextHeartBeatInterval
 - ResourceTrackerService:
    -- If nextHeartBeatInterval is negative, throw exception and crash the RM.
    -- Once you do the above, the setters and getters are not needed, we can for now just set the same interval in every heartbeat directly.

1. Remove setter
2. Remove the extra heartbeatInterval value checking at nodeHeartbeat()

Good catch on the test issue! I think it is useful to start NMs immediately instead of waiting for one second in the beginning - clusters can start up faster. We can move the sleep all the way down instead of sleeping upfront and remove the nextHeartbeatInterval initialization completely.

We should add a new org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.utils.YarnServerBuilderUtils and add a method newNodeHeartBeatResponse method that everybody uses - that would have caught the setNextHeartbeatInterval bug.

And one final thing, the changes related to mockNMMetric seem spurious, remove them?

1. add new java file org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.utils.YarnServerBuilderUtils, add new method  newNodeHeartBeatResponse, then we call this method to create NodeHeartbeatResponse instance
2. remove nexrtHeartBeatInterval initialization from NodeStatusUpdaterImpl.
3. move the sleep all way down to the final block

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

comments.body: This patch simply sends a heartbeat interval T to the NM from the RM in the heartbeat response. The value of T will be read from config. NM uses the value of T to schedule the next heartbeat. The very first NM heartbeat is sent as soon as possible after NM startup (which is what the code should already be doing currently).The accept/reject or dynamic heartbeat interval will be in next subtask.

Thanks for the early patch. Overall approach looks ok. Some comments.

getNextHeartbeatInterval() or simply getHeartbeatInterval() may be better names.
nextHeartbeatInterval or simply heartbeatInterval may be better names. 

please try to adhere to existing code style (similar to http://portals.apache.org/development/code-standards.html) Dont use tabs. Use 2 spaces for tabs and for indentation.

why is new exception being thrown in nodestatusUpdaterImpl?
{code}
+  private void registerWithRM() throws YarnRemoteException,IOException {
{code}

you would want to remove the existing NM-RM heartbeat config or mark is deprecated. heartbeatInterval can become a local variable initialized to some small hard-coded value.

Doesnt look like there is a need for RMTraffic. ResourceTrackerService can read default heartbeat interval from config during init() and then use that value.

The PBImpl changes seem incomplete since you still need to add the new field to the actual proto definition in yarn_server_common_protos.proto. And please look at existing long member values for guidance on other changes.

The expectation is to make the heartbeat interval dynamic, we should remove the old config and not even add in the new config? May be hard code it to 1 sec for now and immediately follow up in YARN-310 ..

I see the config being really a minimum value and not the default. ie. heartbeat interval can be increased under load but RM will try to come back to the minimum heartbeat interval for responsiveness. With that understanding, it may make sense to have it in a config when cluster-size/workload/other-factors make it impossible for the code to achieve a consistent 1 second response time under normal working conditions.
Add the test case to test configuration read correctly.

1. create new contents in YarnConfiguration, to set default value
2. Everytime ResourceTrackerService will set HeartBeatInterval, and NM will get and use this interval
3. add heartbeatInterval variable in .proto file

Comments on the patch:
 - NM_TO_RM_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS should be removed completely, we aren't looking for back compat yet.
 - Rename heartbeat.interval-ms to nodemanagers.heartbeat-interval-ms and move the constants to be together with other RM constants.
 - yarn-default.xml: Fix the new property's description to be so: The heart-beat interval in milliseconds for every NodeManager in the cluster.
 - Rename NodeHeartbeatResponse.getHeartBeatInterval() to getNextHeartBeatInterval(). Similarly the setter.
 - NodeStatusUpdaterImpl: The initial heartbeat interval should be zero. Also rename heartBeatInterval to nextHeartBeatInterval
 - ResourceTrackerService:
    -- If nextHeartBeatInterval is negative, throw exception and crash the RM.
    -- Once you do the above, the setters and getters are not needed, we can for now just set the same interval in every heartbeat directly.

1. Remove setter
2. Remove the extra heartbeatInterval value checking at nodeHeartbeat()

Good catch on the test issue! I think it is useful to start NMs immediately instead of waiting for one second in the beginning - clusters can start up faster. We can move the sleep all the way down instead of sleeping upfront and remove the nextHeartbeatInterval initialization completely.

We should add a new org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.utils.YarnServerBuilderUtils and add a method newNodeHeartBeatResponse method that everybody uses - that would have caught the setNextHeartbeatInterval bug.

And one final thing, the changes related to mockNMMetric seem spurious, remove them?

1. add new java file org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.utils.YarnServerBuilderUtils, add new method  newNodeHeartBeatResponse, then we call this method to create NodeHeartbeatResponse instance
2. remove nexrtHeartBeatInterval initialization from NodeStatusUpdaterImpl.
3. move the sleep all way down to the final block
